description: Mailbox state (kind 30099) test vectors for NOSTR Mail. State uses a G-Set (grow-only set) for reads and LWW (last-writer-wins) for flags and folders. Partitioned by month via the d tag (YYYY-MM). This enables conflict-free multi-device sync.

crdt_model.reads: G-Set (grow-only set). Once a message is marked read, it cannot be marked unread. Union of all read sets across devices.
crdt_model.flags: LWW-Element-Set (last-writer-wins). The most recent created_at timestamp wins for flag state (flagged/unflagged).
crdt_model.folders: LWW-Element-Set. The most recent created_at wins for folder assignment. A message can only be in one folder at a time.
crdt_model.deleted: G-Set. Once marked deleted, cannot be undeleted via state merge (user can publish a new state without the deleted tag to reverse, but that is an explicit user action, not an automatic merge).

vectors.name: mark message as read
vectors.description: Bob reads a message on his phone. The state event is updated to include the message ID in the read set with a timestamp.

expected.note: The new read tag is appended. Existing read tags are preserved (G-Set grows monotonically). The event created_at is updated to reflect when the state was modified.

vectors.name: mark message as flagged
vectors.description: Bob flags an important message. The flagged tag is added to the state event.

expected.note: Flagging adds a 'flagged' tag. Unflagging removes it in a subsequent state update (LWW — the latest state event wins).

vectors.name: move message to folder
vectors.description: Bob moves a message from inbox to the 'Work' folder.

expected.note: Messages without a folder tag are implicitly in the inbox. The folder tag format is ['folder', '<folder_name>', '<message_id>']. A message can only be in one folder; moving to a new folder replaces any existing folder tag for that message-id.

vectors.name: merge two state events (multi-device conflict resolution)
vectors.description: Bob reads message C on his phone (Device 1) and flags message A on his laptop (Device 2) at roughly the same time. Both devices publish state updates. The client must merge them.

state_device_2.note: Device 2 added: flagged(A), folder(A -> Personal). Device 2 has later created_at.

merge_rules_applied.reads: G-Set union: {A, B} union {A, B, C} = {A, B, C}. Read C from Device 1 is preserved even though Device 2 didn't have it.
merge_rules_applied.flags: LWW: Device 2 (created_at 1711850500) is newer than Device 1 (1711850400), so flagged(A) from Device 2 is accepted.
merge_rules_applied.folders: LWW: Device 2 is newer, so folder(A -> Personal) wins over folder(A -> Work).
merge_rules_applied.note: The merged state takes the higher created_at as the event timestamp. Reads use G-Set union (always grow). Flags and folders use LWW (latest created_at wins). This is a CRDT merge — both devices can independently produce the same merged result.

vectors.name: attempt to unread a message (no-op for G-Set)
vectors.description: Bob accidentally reads a message and wants to mark it as unread. Because reads use a G-Set (grow-only), the read entry cannot be removed through merge. The client must handle this as a UI-level override.

expected_merge_behavior.note: G-Set merge will always re-add the read entry because the other device's state still contains it. The read for bbbb...bbbb persists across any merge operation.

workaround.description: To support 'mark as unread' as a user-facing feature, implementations can use an 'unread' LWW tag that overrides the G-Set read status at the UI level.
workaround.ui_interpretation: If unread timestamp > read timestamp for the same message-id, display as unread. The G-Set still contains the read entry (for CRDT correctness), but the LWW unread tag overrides the display state.
workaround.note: This is a pragmatic compromise: the G-Set guarantees convergence across devices for the 'has been seen' signal, while the LWW unread tag provides the UI-level 'mark as unread' feature that users expect from email.
